headline: How European countries are responding to soaring fuel prices
description: Since the outbreak of war in the Middle East, most European governments have tried to limit the extra fuel costs motorists face. While France has sought solutions that do not strain public finances, others have taken a more interventionist approach.

headline: The Gulf States tighten grip on wartime communication
description: Arrests and pressure are mounting in the oil monarchies, which are facing Iranian airstrikes for the first time. Tourists, as well as journalists, are being accused of 'spreading panic' by sharing footage of the damage.

headline: Trump says US bombed Iran's Kharg Island, threatens oil infrastructure
description: Trump announced that the US 'totally obliterated every military target' in Iran's 'crown jewel' Kharg Island, which handles almost all of Iran's crude exports. For now, he said he has chosen 'not to wipe out the oil infrastructure,' but stated if there is any interference with ships crossing through the Strait of Hormuz, he will 'immediately reconsider.'

headline: Eric Fassin, sociologist: 'The Epstein case reveals the absolute power of men who, by flaunting their transgressions, declared themselves above the law'
description: The sociologist describes the Epstein case as 'a circle of male power that is closing ranks against feminist politics.' By exercising this violent domination, money, sexuality, knowledge and relationships become interchangeable forms of capital.

headline: Luxury-loving Belgian cocaine kingpin 'Finger Cutter' to face justice
description: Considered by the authorities in his country to be one of the most important cocaine traffickers in Europe, Flor Bressers, a Belgian with an atypical profile, fond of luxury and architect of sprawling networks, is at the center of a legal saga.

Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
description: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.

headline: An exhibition where art confronts death
description: The Pinault Collection's new exhibition at the Bourse de Commerce, 'Clair-obscur,' features some 100 works by 27 artists that explore the theme of death.

headline: Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
description: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.
